title: pilostyles thurberi
intro: Pilostyles thurberi, also known as Thurber's Cuspidia, is a parasitic plant that grows on the roots of other plants, specifically the stems of Cussonia spp. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antispasmodic and sedative effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Pilostyles thurberi include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's roots and stems in hot water or a solvent, and are often used to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation.
